beats.activity: Wakes up, makes black coffee, reads the newspaper at the kitchen table.
beats.reasoning: Mike's established routine: early riser, disciplined start. Full energy on day one of simulation.

beats.activity: Drives north through downtown to his office in Wallingford.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closure (Westlake–U District) is between downtown and Wallingford; Mike drives anyway as a routine commuter. Early morning, light traffic.

beats.activity: Works at his office: reviews case files, makes calls to clients, handles billing.
beats.reasoning: Quiet methodical work before mid-morning; fits his disciplined, no-nonsense approach to the day.

beats.activity: Drives to a local diner for an early lunch.
beats.reasoning: Stays local in Wallingford; Mike prefers diners and keeps to routine locations.

beats.activity: Sits at the counter, orders eggs and toast, drinks more coffee. Watches the USA vs Wales FIFA match on the diner TV.
beats.reasoning: The 11am match airs during his lunch window; Mike is observant and interested enough to watch, though sports are not his primary passion. He's not glued to it—just happens to be there.
Returns to work after lunch; short local commute.

beats.activity: Afternoon paperwork: follows up on surveillance photos, writes reports for a client.
beats.reasoning: Methodical afternoon work; Mike is meticulous about documentation and follow-through.

beats.activity: Drives south to pick up his granddaughter from school.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon errand to spend time with family; takes route around Light Rail closure. Fiercely loyal to the few people he cares about.

beats.activity: Watches his granddaughter play at the park. Sits on a bench, observant and quiet, occasionally pointing out birds or interesting things to her.
beats.reasoning: Established routine: spends afternoons at the park watching his granddaughter. Sunny weather makes it ideal. A few hours of low-key family time.

beats.activity: Drives south back to downtown, stops at a QFC for groceries and coffee beans.
beats.reasoning: On the way home, picks up essentials. Quick errand, disciplined shopping list.
Evening commute back to home base after a full day.

beats.activity: Prepares a simple dinner, reads a book in his armchair, listens to jazz on the radio. Reflects quietly on the day.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down at home: solitary, reflective time. No unnecessary social interaction. Quiet and disciplined routine before bed.
Full night's rest for a man who wakes early. Seven-hour sleep ends before 06:00 the next day, spanning midnight as required.

diary: Started the day the way I always do—black coffee and the paper at the kitchen table. The newsprint still smells like something real, you know? Drove up to the office through downtown, the city already humming. Knocked out some case reviews and client calls, the usual grind. Took a break at Beth's for eggs and toast around mid-morning. Caught the tail end of the USA-Wales match on their TV. Not usually my thing, but I watched it anyway, nursing another coffee. Back to the office for surveillance photos and reports. The afternoon shifted when I picked up my granddaughter from school. We spent a couple hours at Green Lake Park—she chased around the grass while I sat on the bench. Pointed out a heron to her. Those quiet moments feel important somehow. Grabbed groceries on the way home, some good coffee beans. Dinner was simple. Spent the evening in my chair with a book and jazz on the radio. The city outside my window got quieter as the sun went down. Felt peaceful.
thought_process: The light rail closure didn't affect me today—I was already driving anyway. The morning routine kept me grounded; I needed that. Work was steady but didn't grip me the way it used to. The World Cup match was just background noise, though I found myself watching longer than expected. It reminded me there's a world beyond case files and surveillance. Picking up my granddaughter shifted my whole mood. That park bench time matters more than I let myself admit. I'm not the kind of guy to get sentimental, but being around her clarifies things. By evening, I was ready to slow down—the book and jazz felt earned. The day wasn't dramatic, but that's fine. At my age, steady feels like enough.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
